situation_summary: Primary champion (Head of Product) moved to a competitor and actively recruited 4 internal Apex users to join them. Remaining team skeptical of platform. DAU fell from 29 to 14. One user publicly complained in an internal Slack channel screenshot shared with CSM.
action_taken: Identified 2 remaining power users and invested in them as new champions. Ran 1:1 sessions with each, co-built their top 3 workflows. Set up dedicated Slack channel with CSM for rapid support. Introduced new champions to customer advisory board. Arranged exec dinner with VP CS and Apex CTO.
outcome: Two new internal champions emerged. DAU recovered to 31 in 10 weeks. Renewed with new 18-month contract. CTO became new exec sponsor.
csm_notes: When a champion departs to a competitor and recruits your users, the relationship risk is existential. The antidote is investing deeply in new champions — 1:1 time, career capital (advisory board), and exec relationship building. Never fight the departing champion's narrative — build a better one.
